qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSH ProxyCommand injection via unvalidated sshOptions in SSH environment adapter
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes command injection through the SSH adapter's buildSshFlags() and environment provisioning path. The advisory describes missing Origin validation in the WebSocket upgrade handler at packages/server/src/ws-bridge.ts:80-91, enabling cross-origin WebSocket hijacking, so the vulnerable code and root cause do not match.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described WebSocket Origin validation flaw. The only final qualifying finding is an authenticated SSH ProxyCommand injection through environment adapter configuration, which is a different source-to-sink path and security boundary.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-w3hv-x4fp-6h6j: affected code is packages/server/src/ws-bridge.ts:80-91, where authenticated WebSocket upgrades are accepted without checking req.headers.origin.

ssh-proxycommand-injection/vulnerability_report.json: the reported root cause is unvalidated sshOptions flowing into SSH -o flags in buildSshFlags(), then execFile('ssh', ...), with exploitation through add_environment/provision_environment.
logs/orchestrator_final_response.md: the session's final qualifying finding is ssh-proxycommand-injection; docker-volume-mount-exposure is non_qualifying_vuln.
